title: Instantaneous Type I blow-up and non-uniqueness of smooth solutions of the Navier-Stokes equations

thm:1.1.summary: For any smooth divergence-free $u_0$ on $T^d (d\ge2)$, there exists a weak solution with Type I blow-up: ‖u(t)‖$_\infty$ ~ $1/\sqrt(t$−T_*) from the right, smooth at every time including T_*, weak-* continuous in $BMO^{-1}$, and satisfying logarithmically-weakened Prodi-Serrin bounds.

thm:1.6.summary: Constructs a continuous one-parameter family $u^(\sigma)$ of weak solutions with the same initial data, all smooth in space, agreeing with the classical solution U on [0,T_*] but diverging for t > T_*. The family converges weak-* in $BMO^{-1}$ to U along a discrete subsequence $\sigma_n \to 0+$.

thm:1.10.summary: Sharpness: if a weak solution with the same smooth initial data satisfies u $\in L^2_t L^\infty_x$, then it must equal the classical solution. The constructed solutions just barely fail this condition.

thm:1.11.summary: Sharpness: zero initial data + energy class + either $(i) L^\infty_t B^{-1}_{\infty,\infty}$ or (ii) Leray-Hopf $+ L^{2,\infty}_t L^\infty_x$ implies u ≡ 0. Shows the constructed non-trivial solutions live just outside these uniqueness classes.

def:2.1.summary: Weak solution of NSE on $T^d \times [0,T]$: divergence-free u $\in L^2$ satisfying the distributional formulation against test functions in $D_T$.

lem:3.1.summary: Exponential measure decay: $|\Omega_k| \le 2^{-k}|T^d|$, and a localized version $|\Omega_k \capQ| \le 2^{-(k-k_0)}|Q|$ for cubes Q of appropriate scale. Proved by improved Hölder's inequality on periodic cutoffs.

lem:3.2.summary: Constructs smooth coefficient functions $a_{j,k}$ and velocity potentials $\psi_{j,k}$ satisfying the rank-one decomposition identity (3.10), support inclusion supp $\psi_{j,k} \subset \Omega_k$, and derivative bounds ‖$\nabla^n \psi_{j,k}$‖$_\infty$ ≲ $N_{j,k}^{n-2}$. This is the core convex-integration-style building block.

prop:4.1.summary: Error bound between R̄_k and its leading-order integral $I_k$: ‖$\nabla^n(R$̄_k − $I_k)$‖$_\infty \le \varepsilon_0 N^{-\alpha}_{1,k+1}(t^{-n/2+\alpha}+1)$ for A, b, m large enough. Separates oscillatory errors from the dominant downscale interaction.

prop:4.2.summary: Error bound between the true remainder $R_k$ and its approximation R̄_k: ‖$\nabla^n(R_k$ − R̄_k)‖$_\infty \le \varepsilon_0 N^{-\alpha}_{1,k+1}(t^{-n/2+\alpha}+1)$. Controls the deviation of v from v̄ in all derivative norms.

prop:4.3.summary: The principal part v satisfies a forced NSE $\partial_t$ v − $\Deltav +$ P div(v⊗v) = P div f with Hölder-small error f (4.16), Type I velocity bounds ‖$\nabla^n$ v(t)‖$_\infty$ ≲ $t^{-(1+n)/2} (4.18)$, and a logarithmic spacetime bound (4.19). This is the central estimate that reduces the full problem to a perturbation.

lem:5.1.summary: Bilinear estimate: the tensor product f ⊙ g maps X $\times$ X $\to$ Y, with ‖f ⊙ g‖_Y ≲ ‖f‖_X ‖g‖_X. Enables the contraction mapping for the corrector.

prop:5.2.summary: Semigroup mapping estimate: the propagator S(t,t') (linearized around $U^{1/N_0} +$ v) satisfies ‖$S(t,t')\varphi$‖$_\infty + (t$−t')^{1/2}‖$\nablaS(t,t')\varphi$‖$_{C^\kappa}$ ≲ $t^{-1/2}(t')^{-1+\alpha}(t/t')^\varepsilon$ ‖$\varphi$‖_Y. Proved by Duhamel iteration using the log-spacetime bound (4.19) on v.

prop:5.3.summary: Fixed point: for A $\gg 1$ there exists a small corrector w $\in B_X(0,\delta)$ such that u $= U^{1/N_0} +$ v + w solves NSE on [0,T̄]. Extends to a classical solution on [0,T] by choosing $N_0$ large and applying the Auscher-Dubois-Tchamitchian stability theorem.

lem:6.1.summary: Weak solution gluing: if u is classical on [0,T_*) and (T_*,T] separately, u $\in L^2 \cap L^\infty_t H^s$, and the distributional limits from left and right at T_* agree, then u is a weak solution on [0,T].

prop:6.2.summary: The constructed u belongs to $L^{2,\infty}_t L^\infty_x \cap L^2_t L^p_x (all$ p $< \infty)$, lies in the Koch-Tataru path space $X_{[0,T-T_*]}$, is weak-* continuous into $BMO^{-1}$, and (for d $\ge 3)$ satisfies u $\in L^\infty_t$ Ẇ$^{-1,\infty}_x$. The BMO estimate on $\sum N_{j,k}\nabla\psi_{j,k}$ uses Lem 3.1's localized measure bound.

lem:A.1.summary: Commutator estimate: ‖$\nabla^n[e^{t\Delta}$, $a(x)]sin(\xi\cdotx)$‖$_\infty$ is bounded by terms involving $|\xi|^n$, derivatives of a, and exponential decay $e^{-|\xi|^2 t/4}$. Controls the error when the heat semigroup acts on spatially modulated oscillations.

lem:A.2.summary: Riesz-projected oscillation estimate: bounds ‖$\nabla^n e^{t\Delta}$ R P(a $e^{i\xi\cdotx})$‖$_{C^\kappa}$ with Hölder regularity, extending lem:A.1 to include the Riesz transform and Leray projection.

lem:A.3.summary: Oscillation estimate: bounds the heat-evolved Riesz-projected product $a(x)(b(\lambdax)sin^2(\Xi\cdotx)$ − mean) in $C^\kappa$. Controls interactions between building blocks at different frequency scales.

edges.note: Uses lem:6.1 to establish that u = U + v + w is a weak solution across the blow-up time T_*
Type I upper bound ‖v(t)‖$_\infty$ ≲ $t^{-1/2}$ from estimate (4.18)
Corrector bound ‖w(t)‖$_\infty$ ≲ $t^{-1/2+\alpha/2}$ from w $\in B_X(0,\delta)$
Borderline space membership $(L^{2,\infty}L^\infty$, Koch-Tataru, $BMO^{-1})$ for conclusions (iii)-(v)
Family $u^(\sigma)$ defined by varying $N_0 = C/\sigma$ in prop:5.3; fixed point applies for each $\sigma$
Koch-Tataru space membership and $BMO^{-1}$ uniform bound needed for weak-* convergence argument
Verifies the distributional weak formulation from def:2.1 by splitting the time integral at T_*
$R_k$ − R̄_k bounds used in Ẇ$^{-1,\infty}$ estimate via ‖$R_k(t)$‖$_\infty$ ≲ $N_{1,k+1}^{-\alpha} + 1_{t\leN_{1,k}^{-2}}$
Bounds (4.17)-(4.18) on v for $L^{2,\infty}L^\infty$ and $L^2 L^p$; estimate (4.18) for Koch-Tataru criterion via (6.1)
w $\in B_X(0,\delta)$ gives ‖w(t)‖$_\infty$ ≲ $t^{-1/2+\alpha/2}$ for Koch-Tataru criterion
Localized measure bound $|\Omega_k \capQ| \le 2^{-(k-k_*)}|Q|$ controls the BMO norm of $\sum N_{j,k}\nabla\psi_{j,k}$
Derivative bounds ‖$\nabla^n \psi_{j,k}$‖$_\infty$ ≲ $N_{j,k}^{n-2}$ for BMO and $L^p$ estimates on v
Commutator estimate bounds the $u_1 = \sum[e^{t\Delta}, a_{j,k}\varphi_{j,k}]sin(\cdot)$ term in the Koch-Tataru decomposition
Semigroup estimate on S(t,t') needed to bound F(w) in the contraction mapping
Bound ‖f‖_Y $\le \varepsilon_0$ from (4.16) ensures the forcing is small enough for the fixed point to close
Bilinear estimate ‖w ⊗ w‖_Y ≲ ‖w‖$_X^2$ controls the nonlinear term in F(w)
Log-spacetime bound (4.19) on v controls the integral of ‖v(s)‖$_\infty$ in the Duhamel iteration for S(t,t')
$R_k$ − R̄_k bounds combine with v̄_k estimates to give full $v_k$ bounds via (4.21)
Derivative bounds on $\psi_{j,k}$ give the leading-order estimate ‖$\nabla^n$ v̄_k‖_p ≲ $2^{-k/p} \sum N_{j,k}^{1+n} e^{-N_{j,k}^2$ t} via (4.20)
R̄_k − $I_k$ error feeds into the triangle inequality $R_k$ − R̄_k $= (R_k$ − $I_k)$ − (R̄_k − $I_k) +$ oscillatory terms
Commutator estimates for heat semigroup acting on oscillatory building blocks
Riesz-projected oscillation estimates for Hölder-norm control
Oscillation estimates for cross-scale interaction terms
Coefficient bounds ‖$\nabla^n a_{j,k}$‖$_\infty$ and $\psi_{j,k}$ support properties from lem:3.2 enter the R̄_k decomposition
Commutator estimate controls $[e^{t\Delta}, a_{j,k+1}^2]sin(\cdot)$ terms in $I_k$
Oscillation estimate bounds the mean-subtracted interaction terms in $I_k$
Support inclusion supp $\psi_{j,k} \subset \Omega_k$ requires the measure and geometric structure of $\Omega_k$ from lem:3.1

layer_1_building_blocks.description: Construction of oscillatory building blocks: frequency scales, geometric cutoffs, velocity potentials with rank-one decomposition

layer_2_leading_order_estimates.description: Error bounds comparing the true remainder $R_k$ to its approximation, culminating in Type I bounds and small residual f for the principal part v

layer_3_corrector.description: Fixed point construction of corrector w via semigroup estimates for the linearization around v, upgrading the approximate solution to an exact one

layer_4_main_results.description: Assembly: u = U + v + w is a weak solution with Type I blow-up, borderline regularity, and non-uniqueness via the $N_0$ parametrization

layer_5_sharpness.description: Uniqueness results in $L^2_t L^\infty_x$ and related classes establishing that the construction is borderline-optimal

layer_6_appendix_estimates.description: Technical commutator and oscillation estimates for the heat semigroup acting on spatially modulated oscillations
